sections.subtitle: Recent Form - Liverpool
sections.text: Liverpool's recent form shows inconsistency, with no wins in their last 3 matches (2 losses, 1 draw). Despite this, they remain dominant at home with high expected goals and shot creation (e.g., 28 shots vs. Leicester). However, they’ve struggled to convert big chances recently, missing 10 against Leicester.

sections.subtitle: Recent Form - Crystal Palace
sections.text: Crystal Palace are in strong form, unbeaten in their last 7 matches, including 3 consecutive wins. They’ve shown efficiency in attack, scoring consistently (e.g., 4 vs. Wolves), with Eberechi Eze in top form (6 goals in last 5). Their defense has also held up well against stronger teams.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Analysis
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, results were split (1-0 Liverpool away, 0-1 Palace at Anfield). Matches tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 5 of the last 6 encounters, suggesting a tight game.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Impact
sections.text: Liverpool top the league in goals scored (85) and big chances created (146), showcasing their attacking prowess. Crystal Palace, mid-table with 50 goals, have improved recently but struggle against top defenses. Liverpool’s high penalty area entries (33.7 per game) could exploit Palace’s defensive gaps.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Mohamed Salah (28 goals, 18 assists) remains Liverpool’s biggest threat, while Palace miss key defender Marc Guéhi. Palace’s Eze and Sarr are in form, but Liverpool’s depth and home advantage at Anfield may overpower.
